title: Identify transformations
description: Identify the transformation (translation, rotation, reflection, or dilation) that has been applied to a figure.
topic_path: Topic A: Definitions and properties of the basic rigid motions

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can visualize a line between quadrilateral $A$ and quadrilateral $B$ such that each point in quadrilateral $B$ is the same distance from the line, but on the opposite side, as the corresponding point in quadrilateral $A$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *reflection* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: Concave quadrilateral A has its concave part facing down and to the right and its point facing up and to the left. Concave quadrilateral B has its concave part facing down and to the left and its point facing up and to the right. In both quadrilaterals there is a larger angle and a smaller angle adjacent to the concave angle. In both quadrilaterals, the larger angle is above the smaller angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can imagine holding a pencil on the paper near quadrilateral $A$ and turning the paper until quadrilateral $A$ overlaps quadrilateral $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *rotation* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: An image labeled B and pre-image labeled A of the same quadrilateral. A vertex of the pre-image is at 12 o clock and moves to 2 o clock on the image. Another vertex is at 7 o clock and moves to 9 o clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

Since triangle $B$ is smaller, but has the exact same shape and direction as triangle $A$, we have shrunk triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *dilation* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A triangle pre-image labeled A and its image of the similar triangle but smaller labeled B.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

Since quadrilateral $B$ is larger, but has the exact same shape and direction as quadrilateral $A$, we have enlarged quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *dilation* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral pre-image labeled A and its image of the similar quadrilateral but larger labeled B.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can imagine holding a pencil on the paper near triangle $A$ and turning the paper until triangle $A$ overlaps triangle $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *rotation* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A triangle labeled A and a congruent triangle labeled B. Triangle B faces the opposite way than triangle A.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can visualize a line between triangle $A$ and triangle $B$ such that each point in triangle $B$ is the same distance from the line, but on the opposite side, as the corresponding point in triangle $A$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *reflection* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A triangle labeled A and a congruent triangle labeled B. Triangle B faces the opposite way than triangle A.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

We can move quadrilateral $A$ down and left so that each point on quadrilateral $A$ corresponds with a point on quadrilateral $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *translation* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral labeled A and a quadrilateral labeled B that is the same size, shape, and position as quadrilateral A just moved down and to the left.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

We can move triangle $A$ up and right so that each point on triangle $A$ corresponds with a point on triangle $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *translation* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A right triangle labeled A and a right triangle labeled B that is the same size, shape, and position as triangle A just moved up and to the right.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

We can move triangle $A$ down and right so that each point on triangle $A$ corresponds with a point on triangle $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *translation* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane where a right triangle labeled A and a right triangle labeled B that is the same size, shape, and position as triangle A just moved down four units and to the right three units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can imagine holding a pencil on the paper near quadrilateral $A$ and turning the paper until quadrilateral $A$ overlaps quadrilateral $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *rotation* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane where quadrilaterals A and B are located. Both quadrilaterals are the same size and shape. Quadrilateral B has a vertex that is at 12 o clock, while quadrilateral A has its corresponding vertex at 3 o clock.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What single transformation was applied to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
- A *translation* moves a figure up, down, left, or right, or any combination of those.
- A *rotation* turns a figure around a single point, either on or off of the figure.
- A *reflection* makes a mirror image of a figure across an imaginary line.
- A *dilation* enlarges or shrinks a figure.

For this figure, we can imagine holding a pencil on the paper near triangle $A$ and turning the paper until triangle $A$ overlaps triangle $B$.



[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *rotation* to triangle $A$ to get triangle $B$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane with congruent triangles labeled A and B. Triangle A has a vertex at 1 o clock and the corresponding vertex on B is at 7 o clock.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]
We apply a *dilation* to quadrilateral $A$ to get quadrilateral $B$.

options.alt: A coordinate plane with 2 quadrilaterals labeled A and B. They are similar but quadrilateral B is larger than quadrilateral A.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
